bug_data.description:
There is at least one WPT failure that should be addressed by supplying the SpiderMonkey regex to the URLPattern crate:
`Pattern: [{"pathname":"*{}**?"}] Inputs: [{"pathname":"foobar"}]`

Let's fix this as a followup to the URLPattern interop 2025 project.

comments.author: edgul@mozilla.com
comments.text:
There is at least one WPT failure that should be addressed by supplying the SpiderMonkey regex to the URLPattern crate:
`Pattern: [{"pathname":"*{}**?"}] Inputs: [{"pathname":"foobar"}]`

Let's fix this as a followup to the URLPattern interop 2025 project.
comments.creator: edgul@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
There is at least one WPT failure that should be addressed by supplying the SpiderMonkey regex to the URLPattern crate:
`Pattern: [{"pathname":"*{}**?"}] Inputs: [{"pathname":"foobar"}]`

Let's fix this as a followup to the URLPattern interop 2025 project.

comments.author: edgul@mozilla.com

comments.author: edgul@mozilla.com
comments.text:
Quick status update for anyone following:

The above patch contains an almost working prototype where we provide a rust implementation of Spidermonkey Regex functionality to the urlpattern crate. We create C-API for the necessary SM calls and invoke from rust. Note that the current approach is one that creates the SM RegExp object on demand rather than handling any GC problems created by passing SM GC-controlled objects through rust. This has obvious performance issues that hopefully we can resolve after we get the POC working. 

Note that the above patch depends on upstreaming a small amount of changes to the urlpattern crate. So far the necessary changes are shown here: https://github.com/denoland/rust-urlpattern/compare/main...edgul:rust-urlpattern:tmp
comments.raw_text:
Quick status update for anyone following:

The above patch contains an almost working prototype where we provide a rust implementation of Spidermonkey Regex functionality to the urlpattern crate. We create C-API for the necessary SM calls and invoke from rust. Note that the current approach is one that creates the SM RegExp object on demand rather than handling any GC problems created by passing SM GC-controlled objects through rust. This has obvious performance issues that hopefully we can resolve after we get the POC working. 

Note that the above patch depends on upstreaming a small amount of changes to the urlpattern crate. So far the necessary changes are shown here: https://github.com/denoland/rust-urlpattern/compare/main...edgul:rust-urlpattern:tmp

comments.text:
Created attachment 9538010
Bug 1984171 - Add URLPattern GC incremental slicing for spidermonkey regexp obj r?iain,smaug


This patch adds Trace functions so that when incremental GC happens we can Trace down to the spidermonkey regexp obj that is stored in the urlpattern::component. It's seems a bit complicated because of having to pass through the ffi boundary.
comments.author: edgul@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text: This patch adds Trace functions so that when incremental GC happens we can Trace down to the spidermonkey regexp obj that is stored in the urlpattern::component. It's seems a bit complicated because of having to pass through the ffi boundary.

comments.text:
Quick status update for anyone following:

The above patch contains an almost working prototype where we provide a rust implementation of Spidermonkey Regex functionality to the urlpattern crate. We create C-API for the necessary SM calls and invoke from rust. Note that the current approach is one that creates the SM RegExp object on demand rather than handling any GC problems created by passing SM GC-controlled objects through rust. This has obvious performance issues that hopefully we can resolve after we get the POC working. 

Note that the above patch depends on upstreaming a small amount of changes to the urlpattern crate. So far the necessary changes are shown here: https://github.com/denoland/rust-urlpattern/compare/main...edgul:rust-urlpattern:tmp

bug_analysis.root_cause: Mismatch between SpiderMonkey regex implementation and the URLPattern crate regex handling causing WPT test failures.
symptoms: Web Platform Tests (WPT) failures related to URLPattern matching
reproduction_steps: Use URLPattern with patterns relying on SpiderMonkey regex syntax, e.g. [{"pathname":"*{}**?"}]
bug_analysis.technical_details: The bug involves supplying SpiderMonkey's regex implementation to the URLPattern crate to fix test failures and interop issues. The current prototype creates SpiderMonkey RegExp objects on demand from Rust via C-API without proper GC handling, causing performance issues and crashes. Multiple patches were attached to progressively integrate SpiderMonkey regex into URLPattern, fix crashes, and pass tests.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Potential test failures for web developers relying on URLPattern, and crashes affecting browser stability during URL pattern matching operations.

common_patterns: Involvement of SpiderMonkey regex handling or related JS engine finalization
Crashes triggered during garbage collection or object finalization phases
potential_root_causes: Incorrect or unsafe finalization of JS objects related to regex or URLPattern
Use-after-free or use-after-finalize bugs in SpiderMonkey's regex implementation
Mismatch between expected and actual lifecycle management of JS objects involved in URLPattern
relevant_fix_clues: Use of SpiderMonkey regex in URLPattern points to integration of new regex engine APIs
Fixes likely involved ensuring proper rooting and lifetime management of regex JS objects
Potential addition of safe finalizers or changes to JSClass finalize hooks to prevent crashes
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the crashes are related to improper handling of SpiderMonkey regex objects during the finalization phase in the JS engine. Specifically, the integration of SpiderMonkey regex within URLPattern appears to expose issues in object lifecycle management, leading to crashes in JSClass::doFinalize. Proper rooting and safer finalization procedures are key to resolving these crashes.

common_patterns: Involvement of SpiderMonkey regex handling or related JS engine finalization

potential_root_causes: Incorrect or unsafe finalization of JS objects related to regex or URLPattern

Mismatch between expected and actual lifecycle management of JS objects involved in URLPattern
relevant_fix_clues: Use of SpiderMonkey regex in URLPattern points to integration of new regex engine APIs
Fixes likely involved ensuring proper rooting and lifetime management of regex JS objects

similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that the crashes are related to improper handling of SpiderMonkey regex objects during the finalization phase in the JS engine. Specifically, the integration of SpiderMonkey regex within URLPattern appears to expose issues in object lifecycle management, leading to crashes in JSClass::doFinalize. Proper rooting and safer finalization procedures are key to resolving these crashes.

refined_analysis.root_cause: SpiderMonkey RegExp JS objects created on-demand via Rust C-API in toolkit/components/urlpattern/URLPattern.cpp lack proper rooting and GC-safe finalization, causing use-after-free crashes and WPT failures.
symptoms: Web Platform Tests failures in URLPattern matching
Intermittent use-after-free crashes with stack traces involving JSClass::doFinalize and nsGlobalWindowOuter::PrepareForProcessChange
reproduction_steps: Create URLPattern instances with SpiderMonkey regex patterns (e.g. [{"pathname":"*{}**?"}])
Observe WPT failures and crashes during garbage collection finalization
refined_analysis.technical_details: The Rust C-API code in URLPattern.cpp creates SpiderMonkey RegExp JS objects without stable rooting or proper finalize hooks, resulting in premature garbage collection and use-after-free crashes. Prior patches did not fully integrate SpiderMonkey's rooting API or implement JSClass finalizers needed to manage RegExp object lifecycles safely.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Causes intermittent browser crashes and test failures during URLPattern matching, reducing browser stability and developer confidence.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: In toolkit/components/urlpattern/URLPattern.cpp, locate the Rust C-API functions creating RegExp JS objects (e.g., CreateRegExp). Update these to use SpiderMonkey's rooting API (JS::Rooted or JS::PersistentRooted) to keep RegExp objects alive during their lifetime. In js/regex/RegExp.cpp, ensure that RegExp JSClass has a proper finalize hook that safely releases resources. Validate by rerunning WPT URLPattern tests to confirm no crashes or failures.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: In toolkit/components/urlpattern/URLPattern.cpp, RegExp JS objects created via Rust C-API lack stable rooting and GC-safe finalizers, causing use-after-free crashes and WPT failures. Fix requires updating CreateRegExp and related functions to use SpiderMonkey's rooting APIs and ensure RegExp finalizers in js/regex/RegExp.cpp are correctly implemented to prevent premature GC collection.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The current analysis is comprehensive and identifies relevant components and symptoms, but it is overly verbose and lacks explicit references to specific functions or code locations to guide implementation. The reproduction steps are somewhat lengthy, and the proposed fix is high-level without pinpointing exact functions or patterns to modify. It also assumes familiarity with SpiderMonkey rooting/finalization, making it less self-contained. To maximize AI-readiness, the summary should be more concise, list explicit file and function names where the RegExp objects are created and finalized, and provide a clearer, actionable fix sketch referencing SpiderMonkey's rooting APIs and finalize hooks precisely.

filtered_context.concise_summary: In toolkit/components/urlpattern/URLPattern.cpp, RegExp JS objects created via Rust C-API (e.g., CreateRegExp) lack stable rooting (missing JS::Rooted/JS::PersistentRooted) and proper finalize hooks in js/regex/RegExp.cpp, causing premature GC, use-after-free crashes, and WPT failures. Fix by updating URLPattern.cpp to root RegExp objects during their lifetime and ensure RegExp JSClass finalizers correctly release resources.
core_symptoms: Intermittent use-after-free crashes during GC finalization (JSClass::doFinalize)
technical_constraints: RegExp JS objects must be rooted with SpiderMonkey's rooting APIs (JS::Rooted or JS::PersistentRooted) to prevent premature GC
RegExp JSClass requires a proper finalize hook to safely release resources
Rust C-API bindings must integrate with SpiderMonkey GC rooting and finalization conventions
